name: Xray Test Management for Jira API
what_it_does: Xray REST API for test management within Jira (both Cloud and Data Center). Enables AI agents to manage test cases, test plans, test runs, and requirements coverage directly in Jira. Xray is the most widely used Jira-native test management add-on, supporting BDD/Gherkin, manual, and automated testing.
use_cases: Querying test coverage mapped to Jira requirements from QA traceability agents
Retrieving test execution results and defect linkage from quality analytics agents
Managing BDD/Gherkin test scenarios integrated with development sprints from CI/CD agents
Generating test coverage reports linked to epics and stories from release readiness agents
not_for: Standalone test management without Jira integration
best_when: A Jira-native engineering team wants AI agents to query test-to-requirement traceability, surface coverage gaps, and integrate QA data into sprint planning and release workflows.
avoid_when: You don't use Jira — Xray is deeply Jira-dependent. Use Qase or TestRail for standalone test management.

auth.notes: Xray Cloud uses client credentials (client ID + client secret) for API auth. Xray Data Center uses Jira API tokens. Auth tokens are short-lived (1 hour).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for Jira Free (up to 10 users)
pricing.paid_tiers: Xray Cloud pricing by Jira user tier; included in Jira subscription tiers
pricing.notes: Xray pricing tied to Jira subscription. Marketplace app — paid for Jira Standard and above.

performance.rate_limits: Xray Cloud API: rate limits follow Jira Cloud rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: No public MCP server — REST API integration required
Xray Cloud auth tokens expire in 1 hour — agents must refresh tokens
Xray Cloud and Xray Data Center have different API endpoints — verify deployment type
GraphQL API available for complex queries — REST for standard CRUD operations
Test execution tied to Jira issue lifecycle — understand Jira project configuration first
agent_readiness.security_notes: Test cases may document security and sensitive system behavior. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth client credentials. Data residency via Jira.

security_score_components.security_notes: Security test documentation within Jira. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. Short-lived OAuth tokens.
